entities.name: GREY TO GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: An approach to achieve agricultural progress in semi-arid tropics by embracing diversity and adapting to variable environments

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographic region characterized by low rainfall and high evaporation where ICRISAT focuses its work

entities.name: GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: A historical agricultural movement from the 1960s/70s focused on increasing yields through irrigation and agrochemicals

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal grain that was a primary focus of the original Green Revolution

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal grain that was a primary focus of the original Green Revolution

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The artificial application of water to land to assist in the production of crops

entities.name: AGROCHEMICALS
entities.description: Chemical products such as fertilizers and pesticides used to increase crop yields

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSES
entities.description: External pressures in the environment that negatively impact crop growth and productivity

entities.name: CLIMATE
entities.description: The long-term weather patterns that are variable and uncertain in dryland regions

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: The amount of precipitation, which is often limited and uncertain for dryland farmers

entities.name: WATER
entities.description: A natural resource being developed to capitalize on diversity for agricultural improvement

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: KNOWLEDGE
entities.description: Human resource, including farmer-held information, used to advance agricultural progress

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: Biological material and genetic diversity used to adapt crops to their environment

entities.name: SEED SYSTEMS
entities.description: The processes of seed production and distribution utilizing farmer knowledge

entities.name: PUBLIC-PRIVATE PARTNERSHIPS
entities.description: Collaborative agreements between public institutions and private entities to drive agricultural progress

entities.name: WOMEN FARMERS
entities.description: A demographic group of agricultural producers contributing significantly to the grey-to-green revolution

entities.name: DRYLAND FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers operating in semi-arid regions who lack access to irrigation and agrochemicals
